Name: 1_passed away_anthony_died_jim
Representative_Docs: ['Anthony Duarte Birth: 1980 Death: 2025 Anthony Duarte, of La Puente, California, passed away on Wednesday, October 29, 2025, surrounded by love and family. Born on June 9, 1980, in West Covina, California, to Doreen Cardenas and Gildardo Duarte, Anthony was raised with deep love and guidance, and shared a profoundly special bond with his father Robert Cardenas, who helped shape the man he became. Anthony lived a life marked by warmth, creativity, and generosity. His kindness was as constant as his humor, and he approached every day with an open heart and an unwavering sense of purpose. Anthony’s passions reflected his deep belief in connection — through both community and service. He had a profound dedication to civic life, demonstrated by his years of service on the Hacienda La Puente Unified School Board, his time working for a member of Congress, and his contributions to the California Democratic Party. Through these roles, he championed education, representation, and opportunity for others, always guided by empathy and integrity. Anthony cared deeply for his friends and made a lasting impact on everyone lucky enough to know him. He was the first to reach out when someone needed support and al', 'Anthony Duarte Birth: 1980 Death: 2025 Anthony Duarte, of La Puente, California, passed away on Wednesday, October 29, 2025, surrounded by love and family. Name: 2_family_family members_gifts_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: Family, friends don’t seem to appreciate time I put into tedious Christmas cookie tradition Dear Eric: My late mother made a special Christmas cookie that required tedious decorating, considering each person’s personal preference for toppings, and giving them as gifts to family and friends. They always had a place of honor on our family Christmas table. When she was no longer able, she gave me the cookie cutter, and my daughters and I took over the family tradition. It takes the three of us several days to make, bake, decorate, package and mail cookies to family members around the country. We enjoy doing it and feel like we’re continuing the fun and festive tradition. However, in recent years, not all of the recipients acknowledge receipt. At some point in their lives, they have all helped Grandma decorate, so they know the time involved. Because of past delivery issues, I track the boxes and inquire if I haven’t heard from them in a couple of days. Last year, one response was “yes we got them.” This feels unappreciative! In the past most would text a picture of their children with the cookies, which we really enjoyed. We rarely see any of them anymore, as they are busy raising their families and don’t live nearby. This seems like a way for us to continue somewhat of a co', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Name: 3_travel_tourism_world cup_events
Representative_Docs: ['Sporting And Cultural Events Boost Travel Intentions For 2026 Photo Credit: Deposit Photos Thirty-four percent of American travelers say they are likely to take an overnight trip to attend a sporting event in 2026. Twenty percent plan to attend a FIFA World Cup match, and 52% intend to travel overnight for cultural events such as concerts and festivals. Meanwhile, 47% are interested in touring Route 66 during its 100th anniversary year. These figures highlight a shift for the coming year where travel decisions are increasingly driven by events rather than relaxation. In 2026, Americans are choosing what to attend, not just where to go. The latest Longwoods International study shows that milestone celebrations and global competitions are now major motivators for travel. The calendar is driving demand. “Special events such as the FIFA World Cup, America 250 and the 100th anniversary of Route 66 will all motivate U.S. travel this year,” said Amir Eylon, President and CEO of Longwoods International. The industry has emphasized experience-based travel, but 2026 denotes a more specific trend. Events offer deadlines, shared participation, and reasons to plan trips in advance. The FIFA World Cup brings global visibility and international visitors. Name: 5_museum_egyptian_world largest_tourism
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 6_monitors_monitor_tvs_display
Representative_Docs: ['Luckfox 16-inch 4K 120Hz portable touch monitor offers 100% sRGB coverage The segment of portable monitors continues to grow, with many models available on Amazon at relatively affordable prices, typically between $100 and $150. These screens can be useful for productivity on business trips or while traveling. But portable monitors that combine a high resolution with a fast refresh rate remain uncommon in the mid-range segment. Luckfox wants to address this gap with its new 16" 4K Touch Monitor. The 16-inch display features a 3840 × 2400 (WQUXGA) IPS panel with 350 nits brightness and a 1200:1 contrast ratio. It uses optical bonding technology with 6H-hardness toughened glass and a matte finish to minimize ambient reflections. The 16:10 aspect ratio provides more vertical workspace than the standard 16:9 format. It also supports a 120Hz refresh rate, which is an upgrade over the common 60Hz panels found in many portable monitors, such as the Auzai ME16Z01 or the Arzopa A1M; response times aren\'t disclosed, though. As for connectivity, the product integrates two USB Type-C ports and a Mini HDMI input. Both USB-C ports support audio, video, and power delivery, meaning that only a single USB-C cable is required when connected to a compatible smartphone, tab', 'Samsung OLED 48S95H: New 48-inch and 165 Hz OLED TV revealed ahead of schedule with HDR10 Plus Advanced support Samsung has already teased plenty of devices on the eve of CES 2026. However, the trade show has decided to lift the lid on one of Samsung\'s forthcoming OLED Smart TVs. For context, Samsung listed numerous unreleased Smart TVs on its Parts Database over a month ago. Of those, CES has announced that the S95H is a 2026 Honoree in its Gaming & eSports category. Specifically, the trade show has signalled out a 48-inch variant that was missing from November\'s leak. Incidentally, last year\'s S95F only went as small as 55-inches (curr. $1,997 on Amazon). According to the listing, the S95H offers a 165 Hz refresh rate with VRR technology supported. Assuming a 4K resolution from the S95F series and the usage of HDMI 2.1 ports, a 165 Hz refresh rate would require the usage of Display Stream Compression (DSC) to output at its peak resolution and refresh rate simultaneously. Moreover, the new 48-inch Smart TV will support Samsung\'s new HDR10 Plus Advanced format. Pricing and availability are unknown for now; the full description provided by Samsung and CES is as follows: The Samsung OLED (S95H) is the world\'s most advanced 48" OLED TV for high-end gaming. Combining unparalleled brightness and', "World's largest monitor company designed a 4K 48-inch OLED dual-sided smart gaming display with metal feet, a magnetic power socket, and an Alienware-inspired sound system - but who will actually use it? Sign up for breaking news, reviews, opinion, top tech deals, and more. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful GP TV has unveiled what it calls the world’s first dual-sided 48-inch 4K OLED television, designed for gaming and sports viewing. The display is intended to deliver immersive experiences on both sides simultaneously, with built-in audio and dynamic lighting effects. TPV Technology Group, the company behind GP TV, claims the screen targets premium users who value high-end visual fidelity, as well as the public sector and government institutions. This design feels unusual because most monitors or televisions sit against a wall or on a desk, making a rear-facing display largely unnecessary. Its relevance only emerges if the screen stands in the center of a room, similar to display setups commonly seen in shopping malls - and even then, deploying such a device in public spaces would increase the risk of physical damage, leaving the practical use case for this product uncertain. It comes with an audio system that integrates surround sound, Dolby Atmos support, and sound tracking that follows on-screen action. Speakers with fabric covers on both sides creat"]

Name: 7_new season_episodes_release date_netflix
Representative_Docs: ['Is There a A Man on the Inside Season 2 Episode 9 Release Date & Time? Viewers are wondering if there’s a A Man on the Inside Season 2 Episode 9 release date and time. The much-awaited sophomore season has recently released its eighth episode, leaving fans curious about whether the season is over. Here’s all you need to know. There isn’t a A Man on the Inside Season 2 Episode 9 release date and time. This is because the official schedule of the show confirms that the sophomore season will have only eight episodes. A Man on the Inside is a comedy series created by Michael Schur. It centers on a retired college engineering professor who accepts a new job as an investigative assistant and goes undercover at a retirement home to look for a missing necklace. The show stars Ted Danson, Mary Elizabeth Ellis, Stephanie Beatriz, and more. Yes, A Man on the Inside Season 2 has finished. The second season of A Man on the Inside was released with all eight episodes on Thursday, November 20, 2025. No more episodes will be added in this season. Moreover, the showrunners have yet to announce whether the series will return for a fourth season. There have been no reports of a cancellation either. Comingsoon will bring you the updates as soon as they arrive. In the previous episode, titled “The Wrangler,” the Ghoul and Lucy encounter not one but an entire pack of Deathclaws. After deciding to run for their lives, the two narrowly escape. Meanwhile, Hank experiments with the mind control device on a random person he picked up from an abandoned part of the city, and Norm arrives at an old Vault-Tec office. The episode also featured several flashbacks, in which Cooper is tasked with acquiring Cold Fusion technology. Later, Mr. House confronts Cooper in Las Vegas. So, here are all the release details you need to know about the upcoming Episode 6. The episode’s release date is January 21, 2026, and its release time is 12 a.m. PT and 3 a.m. ET. Check out its release times in the U.S. below: Find out how many episodes will be available to watch in the new season right here. You can watch Fallout Season 2 Episode 6 primarily via Amazon Prime Video. Prime Video is a global streaming platform that offers its members access to its endless catalog of movies and shows. Furthermore, the site also produces original projects, such as The Boys, Gen V, Fallout, and Citadel. The official', 'Is There a The Morning Show Season 4 Episode 11 Release Date & Time? Viewers are wondering if there’s a The Morning Show Season 4 Episode 11 release date and time. The popular drama series has recently released its 10th episode, leaving fans eager to know whether the season is over. Here’s all you need to know. There isn’t a The Morning Show Season 4 Episode 11 release date and time. This is because the season has ended with just 10 episodes, according to the show’s official schedule. The Morning Show is a drama series that delves into the inner workings of a high-profile network broadcast morning news program. The series features a stellar cast that includes Jennifer Aniston, Reese Witherspoon, and Billy Crudup, among others. Yes, The Morning Show Season 4 has finished. The fourth season of the show released its 10th and final episode, titled “Knowing Violation,” recently. There won’t be an additional episode in the coming weeks. However, fans need not worry as it will come back with a new season. In September 2025, Apple TV+ announced that The Morning Show had been renewed for Season 5. Name: 8_immigration_britain_british government_diversity
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Name: 9_washing_households urged_uk_cleaning
Representative_Docs: ['British Gas insists UK households have \'four minute\' showers British Gas insists UK households have \'four minute\' showers (Image: ) British Gas has insisted customers have four minute showers as the UK cold snap continues. The energy giant has spoken out with a guide for customers, as the threat of snow looms over England once more in the wake of Storm Goretti. British Gas said: "Cut your shower time to just 4 minutes and you could save up to £70 a year. Switch to LED lightbulbs they use up to 80% less energy." It added: "Keep track of your spending with a smart meter and spot energy-guzzling appliances. Switching electronics off instead of leaving them on standby can save you up to £55 a year." READ MORE Major UK city plans \'People First Zone\' which will prevent cars from using roads NGO Waterwise\'s Stephanie Fox said: “A shower usually uses between eight and 12 litres a minute. "See if you can swap out your showerhead for a water-efficient one. These add air to the water to make it seem like you’re using more when you’re actually using less.” “We have a Waterwise playlist on Spotify,” suggests Fox. “When the bucket fills up, you see how much water you’re using,” she says. Octopus Energy added: "Getting out of the shower when it\'s zero degr', 'UK households with washing machine in kitchen risk £59 charge UK households have been warned over how much it costs to use a washing machine for an hour. In The Wash looked at 20 of the most popular washing machines in the UK to calculate the average amount of electricity and water used per cycle. The average washing machine cycle uses 0.46 kWh of electricity per cycle, with the average electricy costing 26.35 pence per kWh for the Energy Price Cap (EPC) period from 1 October to 31 December 2025. This means that the average washing machine costs 12.1 pence per cycle. It means if you use your washing machine 220 times a year, it would cost you £26.67 in electricity costs. READ MORE 1.6 million drivers handed £160 fine \'after wanting to follow rules\' The average model uses 47.65 litres of water per cycle, at a cost of water per litre of around 0.3 pence per litre. This means that the average washing machine uses 14.3 pence water per cycle. If you use your washing machine 220 times a year, it would cost you £31.45 in water costs. Adding together electricity and water costs, the average washing machine therefore costs 26.4 pence per cycle to run - or £59 a year. The Energy Saivng Trust advises: "Wash clothes on the shortest cycle (e.g. "quick was', 'UK households urged to use tumble dryers within 10-hour period UK households are being urged to use tumble dryers at certain times - in a bid to lower energy bills. Which? have shared how people may be able to operate their tumble dryers for less. According to the consumer champion platform, cheaper off-peak electricity tends to be available after 10pm and before 8am. Will Owen says households should put their clothes on a spin cycle if there clothes are still very wet when they take them out of the washing machine. He said using the spin cycle will remove excess water, “meaning your clothes will dry faster in the tumble dryer or on an airer”. READ MORE Thousands of Blue Badge holders issued update over free bus travel “If your clothes are still very wet when you take them out of the washing machine, you could make use of the spin cycle setting. “Using the spin cycle will remove excess water, meaning your clothes will dry faster in the tumble dryer or on an airer. He said using the spin cycle could cut your bill by a quarter and save you 23p a load. "So if you’re doing three loads a week, it could add up to £36 a year,” he added. Name: 10_surgical_surgeries_patients_procedures
Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques. Name: 11_florence_near term_meeting_italy
Representative_Docs: ['Florence Press Conference Confirms ECB Remains Comfortable in Its Good Place Even a change of scenery has done nothing to change the European Central Bank’s current wait-and-see stance. Florence, where the ECB met today for its only annual meeting outside of Frankfurt, is apparently also this ’good place’, the central bank’s well-known pseudonym for comfortably keeping interest rates on hold. ECB President Christine Lagarde even called it a ‘magnificent’ place – Florence, that is, not rates. The comments at the press conference confirmed once again that the ECB has no intention to leave its ‘good place’ any time soon. We had feared a rather uneventful press conference. What should we say? We hate to be right… Anyhow, here are a few interesting remarks made by Lagarde: On the ECB’s risk assessment, the introductory statement had the most remarkable change compared with September. Last month, the ECB had still talked about risks to the growth outlook that had become “more balanced”; today, it refrained from qualifying any balance of risk. Instead, the central bank had a somewhat more extensive list of upside and downside risks to the growth outlook. Lagarde even said that some of the downside risks had abated. Representative_Docs: ['Pisces Horoscope Today, March 12, 2026 Pisces Daily Horoscope Today, 12 March 2026: Ganesha says, Pisces, today marks a festive mood in your home as close guests visit, bringing happiness and bonding. Important family discussions will take place, adding to the sense of joy. You will feel content as your wishes start to materialize. However, be cautious with financial transactions today, as there may be a risk of loss. Avoid getting involved in arguments and be mindful while driving. In business, you may spend the day handling maintenance or organizational tasks at work. It’s crucial to manage your belongings carefully and stay on top of your responsibilities. Important information could come your way from a colleague, but make sure all your documents are in order. In love, the home environment will be peaceful, with positive energy shared between all family members. Health-wise, you may experience muscle strains or pain, so focus on regular exercise and yoga. Positive– After a long time, there will be a festive atmosphere due to the arrival of close friends at the house. All the members will feel happy to be with each other. And there will also be discussions on some important issues. The mind will be happy after the co', 'Not just polls, you may soon see interactive quizzes on WhatsApp Channels: Details WhatsApp has launched several new features this year, whether it be to make the app more secure or more fun to use. As the year is about to end, the messaging app is preparing to add yet another feature focused on improving interaction within the Channels section. Currently in the beta phase, the feature is expected to be available on both Android and iOS. It will allow channel admins to connect more effectively with their audience by enabling them to share interactive content. While Channels already includes a polls feature, this upcoming quiz feature is designed to be more engaging and knowledge-based, setting it apart from existing options. According to a recent report by WABetaInfo, WhatsApp may soon release a new feature for WhatsApp Channels. As per the report, the feature is currently in the beta stage and is available to beta testers on Android and iOS devices. The rumoured new feature will allow channel admins to create quizzes that followers can take directly within the channel. Much like the polls feature, admins will be able to add a question and provide multiple answer options. Furthermore, they will also have the option to include images with each answer, which can be', 'Soon you can share WhatsApp Status only with Close Friends New Delhi: Privacy controls on WhatsApp may soon get more personal. A new Android beta update suggests the company is preparing a feature that lets users choose a Close Friends list for Status updates, giving people tighter control over who sees what. The discovery comes from WABetaInfo, which tracks upcoming WhatsApp features through beta releases. The latest Android beta version 2.26.5.13, now available via the Google Play Beta Program, hints that WhatsApp is actively building this option, though it is not yet available for testing. WhatsApp working on Close Friends list for Status privacy | Image source: WaBetaInfo According to WABetaInfo, WhatsApp is working on a feature that will allow users to select specific contacts as Close Friends for Status sharing. Once available, users will be able to decide whether a Status update goes to everyone, selected contacts, or only to their Close Friends. More accurately, WABetaInfo notes that users will first need to choose who they consider Close Friends.
